channel.title: Switch to babel-preset-modules instead of babel-env
channel.description:
**--- [Roadmap Card](https://www.notion.so/frontity/Switch-to-babel-preset-modules-instead-of-babel-env-403b4396fc5c4bef8cd5c5a9f76c93d4)**
<!-- Please, follow this template in order to be as efficient as possible. Before opening a new feature, please check that it hasn't been already added. -->
## Description We are transpiling two bundles, one for es6 and another for old browsers. Currently, we use `babel-env` with the `targests.esmodule` option, but the babel guys are working on an improved version: https://github.com/babel/preset-modules
## User Stories
> As a **Frontity user** > I want to **get the best performance possible out of my es6 bundles by default** > so that **my site loads fast in modern browsers**
## Possible solution
Just switch to `babel-preset-modules` when `target === "module"` in our babel file:
https://github.com/frontity/frontity/blob/dev/packages/core/src/config/babel/index.ts#L48-L53

item.title: Switch to babel-preset-modules instead of babel-env
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
— Roadmap Card

Description

We are transpiling two bundles, one for es6 and another for old browsers. Currently, we use babel-env with the targests.esmodule option, but the babel guys are working on an improved version:

GitHub

babel/preset-modules

A Babel preset that targets modern browsers by fixing engine bugs (will be merged into preset-env eventually) - babel/preset-modules

User Stories

As a Frontity user
I want to get the best performance possible out of my es6 bundles by default
so that my site loads fast in modern browsers

Possible solution

Just switch to babel-preset-modules when target === "module" in our babel file:

github.com

frontity/frontity/blob/dev/packages/core/src/config/babel/index.ts#L48-L53

    

  1. "@babel/preset-env",
    2. 

  2. {
    3. 

  3.   targets: targets[target],
    4. 

  4.   useBuiltIns: target === "es5" && "entry",
    5. 

  5.   corejs: target === "es5" && "3",
    6. 

  6.   modules: false,
    7.

Read full topic
item.source: Switch to babel-preset-modules instead of babel-env
